project.title: EXTL3ΔN purification from transgenic HEK
project.projectDescription: Experiment for label-free quantification of contaminants in preparations of EXTL3ΔN from transgenic HEK cells. EXTL3ΔN was purified from transgenic HEK cells by HisTrap and size exclusion before analysing by mass spectrometry.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Protein reduced and alkylated before digestion with trypsin. Peptides separated by LC-MS/MS.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Data processed using Protein Discoverer. All data searched against cRAP, Uniprot human, and SWISS-Prot human databases using MASCOT. Peak areas for each peptide were combined to calculate protein abundance.

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: Department of Biochemistry, University of Cambridge, United Kingdom
labPIs.name: Paul Dupree

references.referenceLine: Wilson LFL, Dendooven T, Hardwick SW, Echevarría-Poza A, Tryfona T, Krogh KBRM, Chirgadze DY, Luisi BF, Logan DT, Mani K, Dupree P. The structure of EXTL3 helps to explain the different roles of bi-domain exostosins in heparan sulfate synthesis. Nat Commun. 2022 13(1):3314

Characteristics[CellLine]: human embryonic kidney cells (expressing the Epstein-Barr virus nuclear antigen-1; EBNA 293)
Characteristics[Specimen]: Secreted EXTL3ΔN was then purified from filtered culture medium by nickel affinity chromatography (using a 1 ml HisTrap column; Cytiva, Marlborough, MA, USA) and size exclusion chromatography (using a Superdex® 200 Increase 10/300 GL column or a HiLoad 16/60 Superdex® 200 pg column; Cytiva) in a buffer containing 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 6.8, 100 mM NaCl, and 50 mM KCl.
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: sequencing-grade trypsin (Promega, Madison, WI, USA) overnight at 37 °C
Comment[Instrument]: Dionex Ultimate 3000 RSLC nanoUPLC (Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc, Waltham, MA, USA) system
Q Exactive Orbitrap mass spectrometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc, Waltham, MA, USA)
Comment[Separation]: Separation of peptides was performed by reverse-phase chromatography at a flow rate of 300 nl min−1 and a Thermo Scientific reverse-phase nano Easy-spray column (Thermo Scientific PepMap C18, 2 µm particle size, 100 Å pore size, 75 µm i.d. × 50 cm length)
Comment[GradientTime]: The linear gradient employed was 2–40% B in 30 min. Further wash and equilibration steps gave a total run time of 60 min.
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer]: All m/z values of eluting ions were measured in an Orbitrap mass analyzer, set at a resolution of 35,000
measurement of the resulting fragment ions was performed in the Orbitrap analyser, set at a resolution of 17,500
